pending.body:
finding_summary: `claim_bead_in_br` now treats any recovered flush plus `br show` reporting `in_progress` as proof that our earlier claim succeeded. At `src/cli/project.rs:979-989`, the new short-circuit no longer checks whether the recovered flush actually contained `update_status(bead_id, in_progress)`. If the repo merely had unrelated pending mutations and another operator already claimed this bead, this path returns `Ok(())`, so Ralph proceeds to create a project and keep the controller in a claimed state for a bead we do not own.
severity: high
affected_files: ["src/cli/project.rs"]
remediation_hint: Only skip the explicit `br update` when `sync_if_dirty()` proves the recovered flush included this bead's `update_status(..., in_progress)` record; otherwise fall through to a real claim attempt and surface the collision.

finding_summary: `handle_propose_new_bead` health-gates the entire function before any of its idempotent recovery branches. The upfront check at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3020` runs before the read-only reuse/reclassification paths at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3023-3159`, which can return an already-created bead or record a planned-elsewhere mapping without issuing any new `br` mutation. On replay after a previous successful create, a conflicted or temporarily missing `.beads/issues.jsonl` now causes the whole amendment to fail instead of reusing the existing result.
severity: medium
affected_files: ["src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"]
remediation_hint: Move the beads-health guard down to the branches that are actually about to call `BrMutationAdapter` mutation methods (`add_dependency`, `create_bead`, `sync_flush`), so non-mutating recovery/bookkeeping paths remain idempotent.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 10 -> Plan and Implement
